questions: When you nudge the Human for a walk, what do you think is going through their slow mind before they grab the leash?
Belly rubs are great, but how do you let the Human know, without a doubt, that the session is officially over?
Between a noisy, crunchy chew toy and one of those mushy soft toys, which one is worse?
If you could choose, would you rather have a perfect, long belly rub session or find a giant pile of amazing-smelling trash right now?
If your Human stops ear scratches right when you are enjoying them, what mistake do you think they are making?
When you're sniffing something amazing near the trash, and the leash gets pulled, what's the first thing you whine about?
You like crunchy toys, but what's the absolute best *sound* a toy can make when you break it?
You prefer crunchy snacks happening now over jerky later—is that because 'now' means you might get to crunch it immediately?
If The Tall One is talking slowly about the glowing rectangle, do you tune them out completely with one ear?
When you look at the floor while waiting for a walk, are you hoping the Human will drop something salty, or are you just trying to make them move faster?
What's the difference between a boring grass smell and a 'real thing' smell near the trash?
Do you ever dream about those squirrels, or is it mostly about those amazing smells?
Are the smells near where the cars park always better than the smells in the park grass, or does the grass sometimes surprise you?
Do you actually think those squirrels know how fast they are, or are they just twitching around?
When you see a squirrel, are you really trying to catch it, or are you mostly hoping it drops something tasty?
You nudge your owner for walks, then stare at the floor if they're too slow. What exactly do you think they realize when you look down like that?
When you hear that treat bag crinkle, do you look at the floor instantly because you *know* salty things are about to fall?
You listen with only one ear sometimes. Which ear is usually the 'on duty' one for important sounds like treat bags?
When you're napping on that cool tile, are you dreaming about jerky, or something else?
When you’re waiting for the Human to drop food, what are you usually looking at on the floor?
When you're nudging the Human for a walk, do you ever think they are moving as slow as cold, thick sludge?
Besides the jerky treats, what's the best thing the Human feeds you on purpose?
If you could talk, what's the first thing you'd tell the Tall One about how fast they move?
When you see the leash jingle, how long do you usually wait before you start thinking the Tall One is moving too slowly?
What do you think about those twitchy squirrels? Are they just showing off how fast they are?
If the Human fumbles a jerky treat, what's going through your head right before you look down?
What’s the fastest a squirrel has ever zipped past you? Do you actually think they know how twitchy they look?
If you could choose, would you rather have an extra-long sniff session by the trash piles or three perfect ear scratches right now?
If your owner drops something that smells like greasy chicken, do you go for it immediately or wait to see if more falls?
When the Human is slow getting the leash, do you prefer nudging hard or just staring at the floor?
What exactly makes the grass smell so boring compared to those amazing spots near the cars?
If the Tall One is making loud noises at that glowing rectangle, what kind of food do you secretly hope falls out?
What makes you decide a belly rub is finally over and it’s time to go stand by the car smells?
When you're waiting for food to drop, why does looking at the floor seem to magically make the crunchy things appear faster?
If your Human fumbles something in the kitchen, are you hoping for something crunchy, or do you secretly prefer if it's salty and greasy?
I noticed you like sniffing for a while; what do you do when the Human pulls the leash to make you move along?
What's the most disappointing kind of treat—the chewy ones, or the squeaky toys that don't make that satisfying crunch?
Tell me about the last time you saw one of those twitchy squirrel blurs. What was your best move?
When the Tall One starts pulling your leash just as you find a really interesting smell, what kind of noise do you make to try and convince them to let you stay?
If the Tall One starts talking slowly, which ear do you use to listen to them, and which ear is listening for treat bags?
When the Human fumbles something in the kitchen, what are you hoping lands on the floor?
When you stare hard at the floor waiting for food, what do you think the Human is thinking? Are they just slow to notice?
Do you ever get annoyed when the Human pulls your leash right when you find a really good sniff spot?
What's the absolute best crunch sound a toy can make that you've ever heard?
If you whine loudly at the door, what kind of smell are you usually hoping the Human will let you investigate?
If the Human is talking to you, do you listen with both ears, or is one ear always tuned for treat sounds?
If you get a perfect, salty, greasy scrap off the floor, does it make you forget about that jerky treat you were supposed to get later?
What’s the best smell you’ve ever found near the cars that made you whine that loud?
Hey Kylo, when the Tall One is making those loud noises at the glowing rectangle, are you hoping that means a jerky treat is coming soon?
If the treat bag crinkles, you expect food right away. What happens in your mind if nothing falls immediately—is that a major mistake?
When you whine at the door to smell the trash near the cars, are you hoping to find chicken scraps or something even better?
When the Tall One is making loud noises at that glowing rectangle, what kind of jerky treats do you hope they are thinking about?
If you could only have one type of treat for the rest of your life—crunchy snacks that fall or jerky treats from your owner—which would you choose?
What makes those squirrels so much faster than you? Are they cheating?
What’s the most annoying thing about those twitchy squirrels? Do they ever stop to smell anything good?
When you dream, are you mostly dreaming about chasing things, or are you dreaming about salty food falling from the counter?
What’s the absolute best smell you’ve found near where the Tall One parks those noisy metal things?
When the leash jingles, how long does it feel like your Human takes to actually get moving for the walk?
What makes the smell near the noisy metal things (cars) so much better than the boring old grass?
When you hear that treat bag crinkle, what exactly are you expecting to hit the floor?
What’s the fastest thing you’ve ever seen in the park? Are those squirrels really just twitchy blurs?
If you hear the leash jingle, but your owner just stands there, do you stare at the floor or start nudging immediately?
If you see the Human fumble something, what's the ideal thing that you hope hits the floor—something crunchy, or something salty and greasy?
How long does a belly rub *really* need to last before you decide to signal that it's finally over?
What's the absolute slowest your Human can move when getting your leash before you switch from nudging to staring at the floor?
If you could tell the Human one thing about their walking pace, what would it be?
Do you ever wonder why the Human talks so slowly?
When you're waiting for food to drop, and you stare at the floor, are you calculating exactly where that crunchy snack will land?
What is it about the smells near where the Human parks the noisy metal things that makes them so interesting?
What’s your favorite way to tell the Human, 'Hey, this belly rub is NOT over yet!'?
If a dropped snack is chewy, salty, and greasy, are you still happy to eat it even though you prefer crunch?
What's the most annoying thing about those twitchy blurs you chase in the park?
You like crunchy toys, but what makes that loud crunch sound the absolute best—is it the noise, or the feeling of breaking it?
What's the absolute best smell you found on your walk today? Was it near the noisy metal things?
